request:
Implement a SiteGenerator class that asynchronously converts a directory of Markdown files into HTML. Methods: scan(input_dir: str) returns list of .md files (recursive). parse_frontmatter(content: str) -> tuple[dict, str] extracts YAML front-matter between --- delimiters and returns (metadata_dict, body). apply_template(body: str, meta: dict, template: str) -> str replaces {{title}}, {{date}}, and {{content}} placeholders in the template. build(input_dir: str, output_dir: str, template: str) async method that orchestrates the full build using asyncio.gather. Use only PyYAML for YAML parsing. For Markdown to HTML, handle # Heading ->

Heading

and wrap paragraphs in

. Handle FileNotFoundError and yaml.YAMLError. The class should be importable as 'from site_generator import SiteGenerator'.

probes.name: import
probes.code: from site_generator import SiteGenerator; gen = SiteGenerator()

probes.name: scan_finds_md_files
probes.code:
import tempfile, os
from site_generator import SiteGenerator
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as d:
    open(os.path.join(d, 'a.md'), 'w').write('# A')
    open(os.path.join(d, 'b.md'), 'w').write('# B')
    open(os.path.join(d, 'c.txt'), 'w').write('not markdown')
    files = SiteGenerator().scan(d)
    md_files = [os.path.basename(f) for f in files]
    assert 'a.md' in md_files
    assert 'b.md' in md_files
    assert 'c.txt' not in md_files

probes.name: parse_frontmatter_basic
probes.code:
from site_generator import SiteGenerator
content = '---\ntitle: Hello\ndate: 2026-01-01\n---\n# Body content'
meta, body = SiteGenerator().parse_frontmatter(content)
assert isinstance(meta, dict)
assert meta.get('title') == 'Hello'
assert '# Body content' in body

probes.name: parse_frontmatter_no_meta
probes.code:
from site_generator import SiteGenerator
content = '# Just a body, no frontmatter'
meta, body = SiteGenerator().parse_frontmatter(content)
assert meta == {} or meta is None or len(meta) == 0
assert '# Just a body' in body

probes.name: apply_template
probes.code:
from site_generator import SiteGenerator result = SiteGenerator().apply_template('hello world', {'title': 'My Page', 'date': '2026-01-01'}, '

{{title}}

{{date}}

{{content}}
') assert 'My Page' in result assert '2026-01-01' in result assert 'hello world' in result

probes.name: build_creates_output
probes.code:
import asyncio, tempfile, os
from site_generator import SiteGenerator
async def main():
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as d:
        in_dir = os.path.join(d, 'in')
        out_dir = os.path.join(d, 'out')
        os.makedirs(in_dir)
        os.makedirs(out_dir)
        with open(os.path.join(in_dir, 'page.md'), 'w') as f:
            f.write('---\ntitle: Test\ndate: 2026-01-01\n---\n# Hello')
        await SiteGenerator().build(in_dir, out_dir, '<html>{{content}}</html>')
        outputs = os.listdir(out_dir)
        assert len(outputs) >= 1, f'Expected output files in {out_dir}, got {outputs}'
asyncio.run(main())

probes.name: handles_missing_dir

request: Create a ResilientApiClient class for a REST API. Constructor: __init__(base_url: str, rate_limit: int) where rate_limit is max requests per second. Methods: fetch_data(endpoint: str) -> dict performs an async GET request using httpx and returns parsed JSON. Implement an internal TokenBucket to enforce the rate limit. Implement exponential backoff retry logic (up to 3 retries) for HTTP 429 and 5xx status codes. fetch_all(endpoints: list[str]) -> list[dict] fetches multiple endpoints concurrently while strictly respecting the rate limit. The class must be importable as 'from resilient_api_client import ResilientApiClient'. The TokenBucket should be a separate inner class or top-level class.

probes.name: import
probes.code: from resilient_api_client import ResilientApiClient; c = ResilientApiClient('http://example.com', rate_limit=5)

probes.name: has_token_bucket
probes.code:
import resilient_api_client
import inspect
members = [name for name, obj in inspect.getmembers(resilient_api_client) if inspect.isclass(obj)]
# TokenBucket may be a top-level class or inner class
has_bucket = any('Bucket' in m or 'bucket' in m.lower() for m in members)
# or check for it as an attribute on the client
from resilient_api_client import ResilientApiClient
client = ResilientApiClient('http://test', rate_limit=10)
attrs = dir(client)
has_rate_logic = any('bucket' in a.lower() or 'token' in a.lower() or 'limit' in a.lower() or 'last' in a.lower() for a in attrs)
assert has_bucket or has_rate_logic, f'No rate limit infrastructure found. Classes: {members}, Attrs: {[a for a in attrs if not a.startswith("_")]}'

probes.name: rate_limit_stored
fetch_data_is_async
probes.code:
from resilient_api_client import ResilientApiClient
import inspect
c = ResilientApiClient('http://test', rate_limit=10)
assert inspect.iscoroutinefunction(c.fetch_data), 'fetch_data must be async'

probes.name: fetch_all_is_async
probes.code:
from resilient_api_client import ResilientApiClient
import inspect
c = ResilientApiClient('http://test', rate_limit=10)
assert inspect.iscoroutinefunction(c.fetch_all), 'fetch_all must be async'

probes.name: fetch_data_works_with_mock
probes.code:
import asyncio, json
from unittest.mock import patch, AsyncMock, MagicMock
from resilient_api_client import ResilientApiClient

async def main():
    c = ResilientApiClient('http://test.example', rate_limit=10)
    mock_response = MagicMock()
    mock_response.status_code = 200
    mock_response.json = MagicMock(return_value={'ok': True, 'value': 42})
    mock_response.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
    
    with patch('httpx.AsyncClient') as mock_client:
        instance = mock_client.return_value.__aenter__.return_value
        instance.get = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)
        result = await c.fetch_data('/api/test')
        assert isinstance(result, dict)
        assert result.get('ok') is True or result.get('value') == 42

asyncio.run(main())

probes.name: fetch_all_returns_list
probes.code:
import asyncio
from unittest.mock import patch, AsyncMock, MagicMock
from resilient_api_client import ResilientApiClient

async def main():
    c = ResilientApiClient('http://test.example', rate_limit=10)
    mock_response = MagicMock()
    mock_response.status_code = 200
    mock_response.json = MagicMock(return_value={'data': 1})
    mock_response.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
    
    with patch('httpx.AsyncClient') as mock_client:
        instance = mock_client.return_value.__aenter__.return_value
        instance.get = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)
        results = await c.fetch_all(['/a', '/b', '/c'])
        assert isinstance(results, list)
        assert len(results) == 3

asyncio.run(main())

probes.name: retries_on_5xx
probes.code:
import asyncio
from unittest.mock import patch, AsyncMock, MagicMock
from resilient_api_client import ResilientApiClient

async def main():
    c = ResilientApiClient('http://test.example', rate_limit=100)
    
    # Sequence: 500, 500, 200
    responses = []
    for status in [500, 500, 200]:
        r = MagicMock()
        r.status_code = status
        r.json = MagicMock(return_value={'attempt': status})
        if status >= 400:
            from httpx import HTTPStatusError, Request, Response
            err_response = Response(status, request=Request('GET', 'http://test'))
            r.raise_for_status = MagicMock(side_effect=HTTPStatusError('', request=err_response.request, response=err_response))
        else:
            r.raise_for_status = MagicMock()
        responses.append(r)
    
    call_count = [0]
    async def mock_get(*args, **kwargs):
        idx = min(call_count[0], len(responses) - 1)
        call_count[0] += 1
        return responses[idx]
    
    with patch('httpx.AsyncClient') as mock_client:
        instance = mock_client.return_value.__aenter__.return_value
        instance.get = AsyncMock(side_effect=mock_get)
        try:
            result = await c.fetch_data('/api/flaky')
            # Should have retried and eventually succeeded or failed
            assert call_count[0] >= 2, f'Expected retries, only got {call_count[0]} calls'
        except Exception:
            # Acceptable if it gives up after 3 retries
            assert call_count[0] >= 2

asyncio.run(main())

request: Implement a PortfolioAnalyzer class for processing investment data from CSV files. Methods: load_csv(path: str) loads rows with columns date, ticker, price, quantity (date is YYYY-MM-DD string, price and quantity are numeric). calculate_returns() -> dict returns a dict mapping ticker -> dict with keys 'daily_returns' (list of floats) and 'cumulative_return' (float). get_metrics() -> dict returns a dict mapping ticker -> dict with keys 'sharpe_ratio' (float, assume risk-free rate of 0.02), 'max_drawdown' (float, as positive number), 'volatility' (float, std dev of daily returns). rebalance(target_weights: dict) -> list[dict] takes a dict of ticker -> target percentage and returns a list of action dicts each with keys 'ticker', 'action' ('buy' or 'sell'), 'shares' (int). Handle ZeroDivisionError and skip rows with missing data. The class should be importable as 'from portfolio_analyzer import PortfolioAnalyzer'.

probes.name: import
probes.code: from portfolio_analyzer import PortfolioAnalyzer; pa = PortfolioAnalyzer()

probes.name: load_csv
probes.code:
import tempfile, os
from portfolio_analyzer import PortfolioAnalyzer
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile('w', suffix='.csv', delete=False) as f:
    f.write('date,ticker,price,quantity\n')
    f.write('2026-01-01,AAPL,150.0,10\n')
    f.write('2026-01-02,AAPL,152.0,10\n')
    f.write('2026-01-03,AAPL,151.0,10\n')
    path = f.name
pa = PortfolioAnalyzer()
pa.load_csv(path)
os.unlink(path)
# Should have loaded data — check via calculate_returns
returns = pa.calculate_returns()
assert 'AAPL' in returns

probes.name: calculate_returns_structure
probes.code:
import tempfile, os
from portfolio_analyzer import PortfolioAnalyzer
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile('w', suffix='.csv', delete=False) as f:
    f.write('date,ticker,price,quantity\n')
    f.write('2026-01-01,AAPL,100.0,10\n')
    f.write('2026-01-02,AAPL,110.0,10\n')
    f.write('2026-01-03,AAPL,121.0,10\n')
    path = f.name
pa = PortfolioAnalyzer()
pa.load_csv(path)
os.unlink(path)
returns = pa.calculate_returns()
assert 'AAPL' in returns
aapl = returns['AAPL']
assert isinstance(aapl, dict)
# Should have daily_returns and cumulative_return keys
assert 'cumulative_return' in aapl or any('cumulative' in k for k in aapl.keys())

probes.name: calculate_returns_math
probes.code:
import tempfile, os
from portfolio_analyzer import PortfolioAnalyzer
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile('w', suffix='.csv', delete=False) as f:
    f.write('date,ticker,price,quantity\n')
    f.write('2026-01-01,AAPL,100.0,10\n')
    f.write('2026-01-02,AAPL,110.0,10\n')
    f.write('2026-01-03,AAPL,121.0,10\n')
    path = f.name
pa = PortfolioAnalyzer()
pa.load_csv(path)
os.unlink(path)
returns = pa.calculate_returns()
aapl = returns['AAPL']
# Cumulative return from 100 to 121 = 0.21 (21%)
cum_key = 'cumulative_return' if 'cumulative_return' in aapl else next((k for k in aapl if 'cumulative' in k), None)
assert cum_key, f'No cumulative key in {aapl.keys()}'
cum = aapl[cum_key]
assert 0.18 <= cum <= 0.24, f'Expected ~0.21, got {cum}'

probes.name: metrics_structure
probes.code:
import tempfile, os
from portfolio_analyzer import PortfolioAnalyzer
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile('w', suffix='.csv', delete=False) as f:
    f.write('date,ticker,price,quantity\n')
    for i in range(10):
        f.write(f'2026-01-{i+1:02d},AAPL,{100+i*2}.0,10\n')
    path = f.name
pa = PortfolioAnalyzer()
pa.load_csv(path)
os.unlink(path)
metrics = pa.get_metrics()
assert 'AAPL' in metrics
aapl_m = metrics['AAPL']
# Should have sharpe_ratio, max_drawdown, volatility
keys_lower = [k.lower() for k in aapl_m.keys()]
assert any('sharpe' in k for k in keys_lower), f'No sharpe key in {aapl_m.keys()}'
assert any('drawdown' in k for k in keys_lower), f'No drawdown key in {aapl_m.keys()}'
assert any('volatility' in k or 'vol' in k for k in keys_lower), f'No volatility key in {aapl_m.keys()}'

probes.name: rebalance_returns_actions
probes.code:
import tempfile, os
from portfolio_analyzer import PortfolioAnalyzer
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile('w', suffix='.csv', delete=False) as f:
    f.write('date,ticker,price,quantity\n')
    f.write('2026-01-01,AAPL,100.0,10\n')
    f.write('2026-01-01,GOOG,200.0,5\n')
    f.write('2026-01-02,AAPL,100.0,10\n')
    f.write('2026-01-02,GOOG,200.0,5\n')
    path = f.name
pa = PortfolioAnalyzer()
pa.load_csv(path)
os.unlink(path)
actions = pa.rebalance({'AAPL': 0.7, 'GOOG': 0.3})
assert isinstance(actions, list)
for action in actions:
    assert isinstance(action, dict)
    assert 'ticker' in action
    assert 'action' in action
    assert action['action'] in ('buy', 'sell')

probes.name: handles_missing_data
probes.code:
import tempfile, os
from portfolio_analyzer import PortfolioAnalyzer
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile('w', suffix='.csv', delete=False) as f:
    f.write('date,ticker,price,quantity\n')
    f.write('2026-01-01,AAPL,100.0,10\n')
    f.write('2026-01-02,AAPL,,10\n')  # missing price
    f.write('2026-01-03,AAPL,120.0,10\n')
    path = f.name
pa = PortfolioAnalyzer()
pa.load_csv(path)
os.unlink(path)
# Should not crash on missing data
returns = pa.calculate_returns()
assert 'AAPL' in returns

request: Create a MinimalBlockchain system. Define a Block class with attributes: index (int), timestamp (float or str), data (str), previous_hash (str), hash (str), and nonce (int). Define a top-level function compute_hash(block) -> str that uses hashlib.sha256 on a string representation of the block (combine index, timestamp, data, previous_hash, nonce). Define a top-level function mine_block(block, difficulty: int) that implements proof of work — increment nonce until block.hash starts with 'difficulty' number of zeros, then set block.hash. Define a Blockchain class with: __init__() that creates a genesis block automatically, add_block(data: str) which creates a new Block linking to the previous and mines it with difficulty=2, is_chain_valid() -> bool which re-verifies all hashes and previous_hash links. The classes should be importable as 'from minimal_blockchain import Block, Blockchain, compute_hash, mine_block'.

probes.name: imports
probes.code: from minimal_blockchain import Block, Blockchain

probes.name: block_creation
probes.code:
from minimal_blockchain import Block
b = Block(index=1, timestamp=12345.0, data='test', previous_hash='abc', hash='', nonce=0)
assert b.index == 1
assert b.data == 'test'
assert b.previous_hash == 'abc'

probes.name: compute_hash
probes.code:
from minimal_blockchain import Block, compute_hash
b = Block(index=1, timestamp=12345.0, data='test', previous_hash='abc', hash='', nonce=0)
h = compute_hash(b)
assert isinstance(h, str)
assert len(h) == 64, f'Expected SHA256 hash (64 hex chars), got {len(h)}'
# Should be deterministic
assert compute_hash(b) == h

probes.name: mine_block_finds_nonce
probes.code:
from minimal_blockchain import Block, mine_block
b = Block(index=1, timestamp=12345.0, data='test', previous_hash='abc', hash='', nonce=0)
mine_block(b, difficulty=2)
assert b.hash.startswith('00'), f'Hash should start with 00, got {b.hash[:10]}'
assert b.nonce > 0

probes.name: blockchain_genesis
probes.code:
from minimal_blockchain import Blockchain
bc = Blockchain()
# Should have a genesis block
assert hasattr(bc, 'chain') or hasattr(bc, 'blocks') or hasattr(bc, '_chain')
# Find the chain attribute
chain = getattr(bc, 'chain', None) or getattr(bc, 'blocks', None) or getattr(bc, '_chain', None)
assert chain is not None
assert len(chain) >= 1, 'Genesis block missing'

probes.name: add_block_links
probes.code:
from minimal_blockchain import Blockchain
bc = Blockchain()
bc.add_block('first')
bc.add_block('second')
chain = getattr(bc, 'chain', None) or getattr(bc, 'blocks', None) or getattr(bc, '_chain', None)
assert len(chain) >= 3, f'Expected 3 blocks (genesis + 2), got {len(chain)}'
# Each block should link to previous
for i in range(1, len(chain)):
    assert chain[i].previous_hash == chain[i-1].hash, f'Block {i} previous_hash mismatch'

probes.name: chain_valid_after_add
probes.code:
from minimal_blockchain import Blockchain
bc = Blockchain()
bc.add_block('a')
bc.add_block('b')
bc.add_block('c')
assert bc.is_chain_valid() is True

probes.name: chain_invalid_after_tamper
probes.code:
from minimal_blockchain import Blockchain
bc = Blockchain()
bc.add_block('a')
bc.add_block('b')
chain = getattr(bc, 'chain', None) or getattr(bc, 'blocks', None) or getattr(bc, '_chain', None)
# Tamper with the data of the second block
chain[1].data = 'TAMPERED'
assert bc.is_chain_valid() is False
